metadata.purpose: Structured clause inventory for compliance-style Q&A generation

clause_paths: Part A: GENERAL TERMS AND CONDITIONS > FEES > 3.1
Part A: GENERAL TERMS AND CONDITIONS > FEES > 3.2
Part A: GENERAL TERMS AND CONDITIONS > FEES > 3.3
Part A: GENERAL TERMS AND CONDITIONS > ADDITIONAL TERMS > 14.3
Part B: SPECIFIC TERMS AND CONDITIONS > Part I: Specific Terms For Payment Aggregation Services > PAYMENT PROCESSING > e
Part B: SPECIFIC TERMS AND CONDITIONS > Part I: Specific Terms For Payment Aggregation Services > VALUE ADDED SERVICES
topics.legal_commercial_meaning: Razorpay may levy, update, and invoice fees for services and value-added services; fees are exclusive of taxes, and fee credits can be used before deduction from transaction amounts.
clear_answer_uses: Whether fees are exclusive of taxes
Whether monthly invoices are available on the dashboard
Whether fee credits may be consumed before deducting from settlements
clarification_triggers: Which service or value-added service is being used
Whether fee credits are available
Whether the dispute is within the invoice notice period
ambiguity_triggers: Exact fee amount, rate, or future pricing change is determined by Razorpay or separate pricing terms rather than the ToS text alone

clause_paths: Part A: GENERAL TERMS AND CONDITIONS > DEFINITIONS
Part B: SPECIFIC TERMS AND CONDITIONS > Part I: Specific Terms For Payment Aggregation Services > REFUNDS
Part B: SPECIFIC TERMS AND CONDITIONS > Part I: Specific Terms For Payment Aggregation Services > COMPLIANCE WITH PAYMENT AGGREGATOR GUIDELINES
topics.legal_commercial_meaning: Refunds are merchant-initiated and subject to funds in the Escrow Account; they route back to the original payment method, Razorpay fees remain payable, and late-authorized uncaptured payments may be auto-refunded within five days.
clear_answer_uses: Whether a refund must go back to the same payment method
Whether Razorpay fees remain payable after a refund
Whether merchants must disclose refund and cancellation policies
clarification_triggers: Whether funds are available in the Escrow Account
Whether the payment was late-authorized but not captured
Whether the merchant initiated the refund on the Platform
ambiguity_triggers: The ToS does not provide complete operational SLAs for all refund rails or customer-facing policy terms

clause_paths: Part A: GENERAL TERMS AND CONDITIONS > DEFINITIONS
Part B: SPECIFIC TERMS AND CONDITIONS > Part I: Specific Terms For Payment Aggregation Services > CHARGEBACKS
topics.legal_commercial_meaning: Chargeback liability rests with the merchant; Razorpay may deduct chargeback amounts on receipt of a claim, request supporting documents, recover shortfalls, and withhold post-termination settlement amounts for 120 days based on stipulated chargeback percentage.
clear_answer_uses: Whether the merchant is liable for domestic and international chargebacks
How quickly chargeback documents must be furnished
Whether Razorpay can issue a debit note for insufficient funds
clarification_triggers: Whether the Facility Provider accepted the chargeback documents
Whether the chargeback relates to an EMI loan cancellation
Whether termination notice has been issued
ambiguity_triggers: The Facility Provider's ultimate decision and any alternate document period may be outside the ToS

clause_paths: Part A: GENERAL TERMS AND CONDITIONS > USAGE OF THE SERVICES BY THE USER > 2.1
Part A: GENERAL TERMS AND CONDITIONS > USAGE OF THE SERVICES BY THE USER > 2.11
Part A: GENERAL TERMS AND CONDITIONS > USAGE OF THE SERVICES BY THE USER > 2.23
Part B: SPECIFIC TERMS AND CONDITIONS > Part I: Specific Terms For Payment Aggregation Services > FRAUDULENT TRANSACTIONS
Part B: SPECIFIC TERMS AND CONDITIONS > Part I: Specific Terms For Payment Aggregation Services > COMPLIANCE WITH PAYMENT AGGREGATOR GUIDELINES
topics.legal_commercial_meaning: Razorpay can manage fraud risk, including blacklisting end users and suspending settlements during investigations of reported unauthorized debits; the merchant is responsible for fraudulent-transaction liability and regulatory complaint cooperation.
clear_answer_uses: Whether Razorpay can suspend settlements during a fraudulent-transaction investigation
Whether Razorpay disclaims liability for fraudulent transactions
Whether complaint/query cooperation is required
clarification_triggers: Whether a Facility Provider has intimated Razorpay
Whether the disputed amount was already settled
Whether the fraud resulted in a chargeback
ambiguity_triggers: Post-settlement fraud disputes cross-reference RBI and NPCI notifications not fully reproduced in the ToS

clause_paths: Part A: GENERAL TERMS AND CONDITIONS > DEFINITIONS
Part A: GENERAL TERMS AND CONDITIONS > USAGE OF THE SERVICES BY THE USER > 2.25
Part A: GENERAL TERMS AND CONDITIONS > FEES > 3.4
Part B: SPECIFIC TERMS AND CONDITIONS > Part I: Specific Terms For Payment Aggregation Services > PAYMENT PROCESSING
Part B: SPECIFIC TERMS AND CONDITIONS > Part I: Specific Terms For Payment Aggregation Services > GENERAL
Part B: SPECIFIC TERMS AND CONDITIONS > Part I: Specific Terms For Payment Aggregation Services > COMPLIANCE WITH PAYMENT AGGREGATOR GUIDELINES
topics.legal_commercial_meaning: Settlement is net of permissible deductions and depends on funds reaching the Escrow Account plus risk/compliance checks; domestic and international timelines differ, and Razorpay or Facility Providers may hold, deduct, set off, or recover amounts in defined risk and liability scenarios.
clear_answer_uses: Whether domestic settlement timing is five days from escrow receipt
Whether international or PA-CB inward timing is fifteen days from escrow receipt
Whether Razorpay can deduct fees, chargebacks, fines, and penalties from settlement
clarification_triggers: Transaction type: domestic, international or PA-CB inward, or PA-CB outward
Whether funds have been received in the Escrow Account
Whether risk/compliance checks or Facility Provider directions are pending
ambiguity_triggers: Razorpay has discretion to withhold or refund certain escrow amounts where settlement is not feasible or suitable; the ToS does not state a universal maximum hold period

clause_paths: Part A: GENERAL TERMS AND CONDITIONS > USAGE OF THE SERVICES BY THE USER
Part A: GENERAL TERMS AND CONDITIONS > ADDITIONAL TERMS > 14.7
Part A: GENERAL TERMS AND CONDITIONS > SUSPENSION AND TERMINATION
Part B: SPECIFIC TERMS AND CONDITIONS > Part IB: Specific Terms Applicable to PA-CB Outward Transactions > PAYMENT PROCESSING > 1.18
topics.legal_commercial_meaning: Merchants may not use Razorpay for unlawful, fraudulent, forbidden, or policy-prohibited goods and services; listed examples include crypto products, unauthorized software, gambling, banned businesses, and categories updated by Razorpay based on Facility Provider instructions.
clear_answer_uses: Whether crypto/NFT activity is prohibited
Whether unauthorized software or gambling-related content is prohibited
Whether sub-merchants must avoid the prohibited list
clarification_triggers: Precise product or service category
Whether the activity is by the merchant or a sub-merchant
Whether Razorpay has approved the merchant business profile
ambiguity_triggers: Borderline products may depend on Razorpay policies, Facility Provider updates, or applicable law beyond the static ToS text

clause_paths: Part A: GENERAL TERMS AND CONDITIONS > ADDITIONAL TERMS > 14.10
Part A: GENERAL TERMS AND CONDITIONS > SUSPENSION AND TERMINATION
topics.legal_commercial_meaning: The ToS expressly bars real-money online gaming or online money game services as defined under the Promotion and Regulation of Online Gaming Act, 2025, and also lists gaming/gambling, sports bets, lotteries, sweepstakes, and games of chance as restricted or prohibited categories.
clear_answer_uses: Whether real-money online gaming payments are allowed
Whether sports betting, lotteries, or gambling-site memberships are allowed
clarification_triggers: Whether the product involves real money, prize value, betting, chance, or gambling-like mechanics
ambiguity_triggers: Classification of a game may depend on facts and external gaming law definitions not fully reproduced in the ToS

clause_paths: Part A: GENERAL TERMS AND CONDITIONS > DEFINITIONS
Part A: GENERAL TERMS AND CONDITIONS > USAGE OF THE SERVICES BY THE USER > 2.22
Part A: GENERAL TERMS AND CONDITIONS > ADDITIONAL TERMS > 14.2
Part B: SPECIFIC TERMS AND CONDITIONS > Part IB: Specific Terms Applicable to PA-CB Outward Transactions > PAYMENT PROCESSING
topics.legal_commercial_meaning: Marketplace merchants must accept payments only for onboarded sellers contractually tied to them; cross-border outward payment aggregators/e-commerce marketplaces can be onboarded as master merchants for sub-merchants, but must maintain permits, evidence authorization, conduct due diligence, enforce prohibited-list and service-term compliance, and indemnify Razorpay for sub-merchant acts.
clear_answer_uses: Whether marketplace sellers must be onboarded and contractually obligated
Whether cross-border marketplace merchants are responsible for sub-merchant acts
Whether sub-merchants must avoid prohibited goods/services
clarification_triggers: Whether the merchant is domestic or outside India
Whether payments are accepted on behalf of sellers/clients/customers
Whether third-party settlement is requested
ambiguity_triggers: Required licenses, permits, seller due diligence scope, and cross-border marketplace obligations depend on jurisdiction and applicable law

clause_paths: Part A: GENERAL TERMS AND CONDITIONS > DEFINITIONS
Part A: GENERAL TERMS AND CONDITIONS > USAGE OF THE SERVICES BY THE USER
Part B: SPECIFIC TERMS AND CONDITIONS > Part I: Specific Terms For Payment Aggregation Services > COMPLIANCE WITH PAYMENT AGGREGATOR GUIDELINES
topics.legal_commercial_meaning: Merchants must provide accurate KYC and business information; Razorpay may conduct due diligence, retrieve CKYCR records, demand extra KYC/customer/invoice documents, monitor transactions, and suspend services or stop settlement until documents satisfy Razorpay.
clear_answer_uses: Whether Razorpay can ask for additional KYC documents
Whether inaccurate information can lead to suspension or termination
Whether Razorpay can retrieve CKYCR documents
clarification_triggers: Whether the requested documents are merchant KYC, customer KYC, or invoices
Whether the merchant failed to submit documents
Whether a governmental authority or Facility Provider requested documents
ambiguity_triggers: Exact KYC document set and satisfaction threshold are discretionary or set by external KYC rules

clause_paths: Part A: GENERAL TERMS AND CONDITIONS > FEES
Part B: SPECIFIC TERMS AND CONDITIONS > Part I: Specific Terms For Payment Aggregation Services > SPECIFIC TERMS FOR SNRR MERCHANTS
Part B: SPECIFIC TERMS AND CONDITIONS > Part IA: Additional Terms For Offline Payment Aggregation And Device Term
Part B: SPECIFIC TERMS AND CONDITIONS > Part IB: Specific Terms Applicable to PA-CB Outward Transactions > PAYMENT PROCESSING
topics.legal_commercial_meaning: Fees exclude taxes; merchants bear statutory tax changes, GSTIN accuracy, TDS and e-commerce operator obligations, SNRR Indian tax exposure, cross-border withholding documentation, overseas tax compliance, OIDAR GST assessment, and LRS/TCS declaration duties.
clear_answer_uses: Whether fees are tax-exclusive
Who must keep GSTIN details accurate
Whether Razorpay is obligated to deduct Section 194O TDS for marketplace operators
clarification_triggers: Whether the merchant is an e-commerce operator
Whether cross-border settlement documents such as No-PE declarations were furnished
Whether LRS/TCS applies
ambiguity_triggers: Actual tax treatment depends on current tax law, merchant facts, certificates, and jurisdiction-specific tax advice

clause_paths: Part A: GENERAL TERMS AND CONDITIONS > PROPRIETARY RIGHTS > 1.2
Part A: GENERAL TERMS AND CONDITIONS > USAGE OF THE SERVICES BY THE USER
Part A: GENERAL TERMS AND CONDITIONS > PRIVACY POLICY > 4.1
Part A: GENERAL TERMS AND CONDITIONS > . DATA PROTECTION
Part B: SPECIFIC TERMS AND CONDITIONS > Part I: Specific Terms For Payment Aggregation Services > COMPLIANCE WITH PAYMENT AGGREGATOR GUIDELINES
Part B: SPECIFIC TERMS AND CONDITIONS > Part IV: Specific Terms For Subscription Services
topics.legal_commercial_meaning: Merchants must obtain and maintain customer consents, remain responsible for Customer Data, notify Razorpay of suspected security breaches within 24 hours, avoid storing sensitive card credentials or payment-instrument data, maintain PCI/PADSS compliance where applicable, and provide explicit consent flows for card tokenization and e-mandates.
clear_answer_uses: Whether merchants can store full card credentials
Whether suspected Customer Data breaches must be notified within 24 hours
Whether explicit consent is required for subscription card tokenization
clarification_triggers: Whether the integration is custom checkout or server-to-server
Whether the data is Customer Data, Usage Data, or payment-instrument credentials
Whether PCI DSS or PA-DSS is applicable
ambiguity_triggers: The Privacy Policy and applicable data protection laws are incorporated by reference and may add obligations not fully stated in the ToS

clause_paths: Part A: GENERAL TERMS AND CONDITIONS > USAGE OF THE SERVICES BY THE USER
Part A: GENERAL TERMS AND CONDITIONS > SUSPENSION AND TERMINATION
Part B: SPECIFIC TERMS AND CONDITIONS > Part I: Specific Terms For Payment Aggregation Services > CHARGEBACKS
Part B: SPECIFIC TERMS AND CONDITIONS > Part I: Specific Terms For Payment Aggregation Services > GENERAL
Part B: SPECIFIC TERMS AND CONDITIONS > Part IB: Specific Terms Applicable to PA-CB Outward Transactions > PAYMENT PROCESSING > 1.19
topics.legal_commercial_meaning: Razorpay may immediately suspend services and settlement for inaccurate information, missing KYC, prohibited or unlawful use, Facility Provider or authority instructions, suspicious activity, excessive disputes/refunds/reversals, and LRS breaches; termination does not release accrued obligations.
clear_answer_uses: Whether Razorpay can suspend services and settlement without liability in listed events
Whether termination ends accrued obligations
Whether chargeback and fraudulent-transaction clauses survive termination
clarification_triggers: Which suspension trigger applies
Whether termination notice has been issued
Whether an offline agreement overrides the default termination-at-will language
ambiguity_triggers: Several triggers depend on Razorpay opinion or external instructions, and the ToS does not define a universal maximum suspension or settlement-hold duration

clause_paths: Part A: GENERAL TERMS AND CONDITIONS
Part A: GENERAL TERMS AND CONDITIONS > DEFINITIONS
Part B: SPECIFIC TERMS AND CONDITIONS > Part IB: Specific Terms Applicable to PA-CB Outward Transactions > PAYMENT PROCESSING
topics.legal_commercial_meaning: For merchants based outside India collecting from Indian customers, Part IB adds specialized operational, regulatory, tax, LRS, and settlement rules that prevail over conflicting general terms; settlement/remittance depends on escrow receipt, risk checks, documentation, tax withholding, currency conversion, and compliance with FEMA/RBI/LRS requirements.
clear_answer_uses: Whether Part IB applies to outside-India merchants collecting from Indian customers
Whether no settlement is made until escrow is credited
Whether Razorpay may suspend LRS-related transactions for suspected breaches
clarification_triggers: Whether the merchant is based outside India
Whether the transaction is PA-CB outward, PA-CB inward, domestic, or international
Whether required tax, transaction, LRS, or customer due-diligence documents were provided
ambiguity_triggers: Final remittance timing, LRS compliance, FEMA obligations, and tax treatment depend on external banking, RBI, and tax-law processes

clause_paths: Part A: GENERAL TERMS AND CONDITIONS
Part B: SPECIFIC TERMS AND CONDITIONS > Part I: Specific Terms For Payment Aggregation Services > GENERAL
Part B: SPECIFIC TERMS AND CONDITIONS > Part IA: Additional Terms For Offline Payment Aggregation And Device Term
Part B: SPECIFIC TERMS AND CONDITIONS > Part II: Specific Terms For E-Mandate Services
Part B: SPECIFIC TERMS AND CONDITIONS > Part IV: Specific Terms For Subscription Services
Part B: SPECIFIC TERMS AND CONDITIONS > Part VI: Magic Checkout
topics.legal_commercial_meaning: Specific service terms and accepted supplemental terms can override or supplement general terms; offline devices, e-mandates, subscriptions, and Magic Checkout each add service-specific onboarding, fees, settlement, withholding, fraud, tokenization, and conflict rules.
clear_answer_uses: Whether specific terms prevail over conflicting general terms
Whether e-mandate onboarding depends on Sponsor Bank registration
Whether offline device fees are governed by pricing terms agreed at subscription
clarification_triggers: Which product or service is being used
Whether the merchant accepted supplemental terms
Whether Razorpay is acting only as a technical service provider for tokenization
ambiguity_triggers: Some operational details are in product pricing terms, supplemental forms, bank procedures, or NPCI guidelines outside the ToS
